title: podman prints unclear "binary not found" warning with netavark backend
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Unclear "binary not found" warning when using the netavark backend in podman 4.0.1. It's not clear what binary is missing. Podman commands seem to succeed despite this warning.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set `network_backend = "netavark"` in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`

2. Run any podman command

3. Command succeeds, but podman prints "WARN[0000]  binary not found, container dns will not be enabled" to stderr

**Describe the results you received:**
With the old CNI backend I get no message printed to stderr:

```console
$ podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}
cni
```
But after setting the network backend to netavark I get:

```console
$ podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}
WARN[0000]  binary not found, container dns will not be enabled 
netavark
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
No warning.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.7
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Thu Feb 24 14:12:50 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Arch Linux on a physical machine.

comments.body: it's probably https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns which is not found or missing

@MarkusSchoelzel, you're right. If I install the [aardvark-dns](https://archlinux.org/packages/community/x86_64/aardvark-dns/) package, which is an optional dependency of the [netavark](https://archlinux.org/packages/community/x86_64/netavark/) package, then the warning disappears.

@cloudlena @alanorth This is correct. Following message is just a warning notifying users that `dns` will not work if `aardvark-dns` is not installed. Did you manually build and install on `Arch` ? I'm closing this issue since its already resolved but lets continue discussion below.

The problem is that `aardvark-dns` is only an optional dependency (instead of a normal "strict" dependency) of `netavark` so it doesn't get installed automatically by Arch Linux' package manager. That is technically correct because `netavark` does work without it (just without DNS).

The question is whether it's user-friendly, because DNS is quite essential for many containers to work. But I think that's a question for David Runge, the maintainer of the `podman`, `netavark`, and `aardvark-dns` Arch Linux packages. I expect most users to be confused about this warning (just like @alanorth and I were) after installing the `podman` package with `netavark`.
@flouthoc could you fix the error message to make it clear which binary is missing.
@cloudlena @rhatdan Above PR fixes the error message.
Thank you. I completely missed that there was an optional package `aardvark-dns` in Arch Linux. Now I know, though improving the warning message will help users in the future!
